task_completion.description: The agent writes or executes a script to programmatically query the OpenC3 COSMOS dictionary.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal or IDE showing a script (e.g., Python, Ruby, bash, curl) interacting with the OpenC3 COSMOS API or parsing its configuration files.

task_completion.description: The JSON dictionary file is created at the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal or file explorer showing `cmd_tlm_dictionary.json` located in `/home/ga/Desktop/`.

task_completion.description: The JSON file contains the correct target and a summary section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of the JSON file show '"target": "INST"' and a summary section containing aggregate counts of commands and telemetry.

task_completion.description: The JSON file documents at least 3 telemetry packets and their items.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The JSON file contains a '"telemetry_packets"' section listing at least 3 valid packets (e.g., HEALTH_STATUS, ADCS, PARAMS) along with their constituent item names.

task_completion.description: The JSON file documents at least 3 commands and their parameters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The JSON file contains a commands section listing at least 3 valid commands (e.g., CLEAR, COLLECT, SETPARAMS) along with their parameter names.

integrity.description: The JSON file was generated by a script rather than manually typed.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent runs a script that outputs the file, rather than opening a text editor and manually typing the JSON structure and data line-by-line.

integrity.description: The data in the JSON file matches the actual OpenC3 COSMOS INST target.
integrity.visual_evidence: The telemetry packets and commands match the known baseline (e.g., HEALTH_STATUS, CLEAR) and are not fabricated or generic placeholders.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply write a hardcoded JSON string to a file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The script contains logic to query an API, read files, or parse data, rather than just a hardcoded JSON string being written directly to the output file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file is /home/ga/Desktop/cmd_tlm_dictionary.json. The target is 'INST'. Genuine telemetry packets for the INST target include HEALTH_STATUS, ADCS, PARAMS, IMAGE, MECH, MEMORY, SYSTEM, LIMITS_VIOLATION. Genuine commands include CLEAR, COLLECT, SETPARAMS, START, STOP, ABORT, IMAGE_DUMP, CALIBRATE. The JSON must contain at least 3 of these packets and 3 of these commands, along with their items/parameters and a summary section.
